title: Lisianthius tenuifolius

This family is part of the order Gentianales and the class Magnoliopsida. Lisianthius tenuifolius is further classified as a part of the genus Lisianthius, which is a group of flowering plants that are mostly found in tropical regions. The species Lisianthius tenuifolius is native to the Americas and is known to have several sub-species. The classification of Lisianthius tenuifolius is based on its unique characteristics and physical features that distinguish it from other plants within the Gentianaceae family.

This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
